label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and passage. The question asks which of the following is NOT a reason why the English language spread to numerous parts of the world. The passage says that the English language was first introduced to the Americas by British colonization, beginning in 1607 in Jamestown, Virginia. Then it says that the language spread to numerous other parts of the world as a result of British trade and colonization elsewhere and the spread of the former British Empire, which by 1921 held sway over a population of 470-570 million people. So the reasons given for the spread of English are: - British colonization - British trade - the spread of the former British Empire (i.e., British Empire's expansion) The options are: (A) British colonization (B) the spread of the former English Empire (C) British trade around the world (D) a large British population We need to determine which one is NOT a reason given in the passage. The passage doesn't mention a large British population as
